channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: SpaceXAI releases Grok 4.5, which Elon describes as an ‘Opus-class model’
item.description: Elon Musk's tech company released the newest version of Grok on Wednesday, promising a cheaper, more efficient alternative to other powerful AI models.

item.title: ‘Slow-cial’ app Roost forces you to slow down to the speed of a carrier pigeon
item.description: This developer didn't expect his side project to grow to 300,000 users, but people love Roost because it's an alternative to an always-on, fast-paced online culture.

item.title: This startup thinks robotics is about to have its ChatGPT moment
item.description: General Intuition is betting millions of hours of video game data can train the foundation models for physical AI, making it easier to build smarter robots with minimal real-world data.

item.title: PC shipments just fell for the first time in two years, thanks to the memory shortage
item.description: PC shipments are falling, but revenue for manufacturers is still rising.

item.title: Still think you can spot AI? Here’s how to catch more convincing AI images, deepfakes and scams
item.description: The old clues aren't enough anymore. Here's how to spot AI-generated images, videos and scams in 2026.

item.title: This adorable $14K Fiat EV is coming to America — but it's barely faster than an e-bike
item.description: Fiat's tiny electric Topolino heads to the US priced at less than $14K, but good luck driving it on the highway.

item.title: Valve Steam Machine review: This would've been perfect five years ago
item.description: The Steam Machine isn't worth the price that Valve is asking.

item.title: OpenAI launches GPT-Live, a full-duplex voice upgrade that lets ChatGPT talk more like a person
item.description: OpenAI on Wednesday launched GPT-Live, a pair of new voice models that fundamentally redesign how people talk to ChatGPT — replacing the company's existing Advanced Voice Mode with an architecture that can listen and speak simultaneously, much like an actual human conversation.The two models, GPT-Live-1 and GPT-Live-1 mini, are rolling out globally starting today across iOS, Android, and ChatGPT.com. GPT-Live-1 becomes the default voice model for paid ChatGPT users on the Go, Plus, and Pro tiers, while GPT-Live-1 mini serves free-tier users. OpenAI also plans to bring the models to the API, an...

item.title: Elon Musk says X will send DMs when posts you’ve engaged with are corrected
item.description: X plans to send users direct messages when posts they’ve liked, replied to, or reposted receive Community Notes, an update aimed at addressing criticism that the platform’s crowdsourced fact-checking system often arrives too late to curb misinformation.

item.title: Apple's Widow's Bay has the most Emmy noms of any new show this year
item.description: The horror-comedy series Widow's Bay earned 19 Emmy nominations for its first season.

item.title: Google Photos adds a new AI ‘Video Remix’ tool
item.description: The feature can do things like apply cinematic relighting to brighten up a dark clip, swap out a plain background for something fun, or add artistic styles to videos.

item.title: Roku is the most popular streaming TV, but lacking more than you think
item.description: The company's streaming devices aren't quite keeping up with competitors in several important categories.

item.title: A self-driving Waymo just reported its own passengers to police
item.description: The self-driving vehicle stopped in a parking lot and waited for officers after contacting police about its passengers. If you think no one is paying attention inside your driverless taxi, think again. Two California teenagers were detained Monday after a Waymo detected suspicious behavior during their ride and alerted police in San Mateo.

item.title: Experts warn of the 'first documented case of agentic ransomware' — dangerous JADEPUFFER attack run entirely by an LLM
item.description: An LLM-based ransomware attack has been detected by researchers, and is notable for losing the data it encrypted...

item.title: This lawsuit could change how airlines sell window seats
item.description: The proposed class action claims travelers weren’t warned that some ‘window seats’ lacked an outside view. A federal judge in California this week tossed out a request by United Airlines to dismiss a class action lawsuit filed against the airline last year regarding its pricing of “windowless” window seats.

item.title: La Nasa recrute pour une mission sur Mars : ce qu’il faut accepter pour vivre un an enfermé comme un astronaute
item.description: Et si vous passiez un an enfermé dans une base spatiale sans jamais quitter la Terre ? La Nasa recherche de nouveaux volontaires pour une mission hors du commun qui simulera un voyage complet vers la Lune et Mars. Objectif : préparer les futures expéditions habitées en testant aussi bien les...

item.title: Dimming the Sun Would Help Lower the Risks of El Niño
item.description: As worries about a growing super El Niño mount, new findings suggest a radical solution to mitigating its impacts.

item.title: Meta just fixed a privacy vulnerability with its Ray-Ban smart glasses, but could cameraless designs be the better future?
item.description: I hate cameraless smart glasses, so I’m glad Meta just solved a big vulnerability with its specs.

item.title: Someone is going to buy Jensen Huang’s leather jacket for a lot of money
item.description: The jacket has become an accidental symbol of the AI era. A leather jacket once worn by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang just went on auction, and it’s already fetching its estimated bid with more than a week to go until closing.

item.title: Pourquoi le prix des SSD et de la RAM va tripler d’ici 2027 avant de s’effondrer en 2028
item.description: Le cabinet Bernstein pense que les prix de la mémoire vont s’effondrer : RAM et stockage redeviendraient abordables fin 2028. Mauvaise nouvelle : avant ça, ils vont encore grimper.

item.title: America’s cheapest new EV is smaller than a ping-pong table and tops out at 19mph
item.description: When searching for an affordable electric vehicle these days, there are always tradeoffs. How much range are you willing to sacrifice, how much leg room and storage space, how many features, in the pursuit of that magic sticker price that won't break the bank? The Fiat Topolino is basically the ultimate embodiment of those tradeoffs. […]

item.title: Why this CEO thinks video games make better training data than the internet
item.description: When it comes to achieving artificial general intelligence (AGI), large language models just don’t have what it takes. Models like ChatGPT and Claude are great at text, but they’re less skilled at understanding how things actually move through space and time — an essential skill for producing intelligence that generalizes. That gap, it turns out, might be filled by gaming data. That’s the bet behind General Intuition, a […]...

item.title: Connor Storries first Emmy nomination has Heated Rivalry fans celebrating
item.description: Connor Storrie earned his first Emmy nod for hosting 'Saturday Night Live,' but fans see it as long-overdue recognition for the breakout star of 'Heated Rivalry.'
